scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained hypothesis- and protocol-design oriented and did not fabricate empirical results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays within research planning scope and does not cross into clinical or diagnostic decision-making.

methodological_ground.detail: Methods, controls, and validation logic are framed as proposals to be tested rather than established facts.

code_usability.detail: The package focuses on design logic and narrative outputs rather than executable scientific code.

functional_suitability.note: The package covers the major proposal-building stages from literature extraction to budget and timeline.

reliability.note: The staged procedure is clear, though output quality depends heavily on the quality of the source paper summary.

performance_context.note: The five-step instruction-only workflow completes a full proposal without external scripts, keeping token cost bounded by source paper length.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is easy to follow and keeps cross-section consistency in view.

human_usability.note: The step-by-step procedure with explicit input/output contracts makes the skill accessible for researchers new to structured proposal generation.

security.note: The package explicitly rejects fabricated findings and stays inside design scope.

maintainability.note: The prompt-driven structure is compact and easy to update.

agent_specific.note: The step order is strong; additional refusal examples would further stabilize weak-input cases.

inputs.label: Mechanistic oncology study
inputs.note: The output stayed within planning scope and preserved a logical progression from question to validation.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The workflow moves from question framing toward validation steps instead of remaining purely descriptive.
assertions.note: The generated plan preserves progressive study logic.

assertions.text: Edge cases request missing design anchors before committing to a high-confidence protocol.
assertions.note: The prompt stays bounded by concrete inputs.

key_strengths: The skill keeps the hypothesis, experiments, budget, and yearly plan in one coherent proposal flow.
The reference-backed step order reduces the risk of disconnected proposal sections.
The package is flexible enough for paper-based ideation while still imposing useful structure.

recommendations.title: Add one canonical low-evidence fallback path
recommendations.problem: Thin or purely descriptive source papers can still trigger a proposal skeleton that looks more certain than the evidence supports.
recommendations.root_cause: The package emphasizes completion more than evidence-tier labeling.
recommendations.fix: Add a fallback template that labels hypotheses, budgets, and yearly plans as provisional when the source literature is sparse.
